videos.description:
How to find cheap flights and travel more: https://travelforalmostfree.com/ref=beforeyougo2 // Here are the best things to do in Quito, Ecuador.

=======================
Fired travel agent wants revenge! Here's the secret to cheap flights: https://bit.ly/3da2cED
=======================

Are you planning a trip to Quito and looking for the best things to do? Look no further! In this video, we will take you on a tour of the top things to do and see, including day trips from Quito. From the stunning landscapes of the Andes Mountains, to the architecture in the Historic Center, we will show you the best ways to spend a day outside of the city.

But it's not just the stunning natural beauty of these attractions that make them so special. Quito and the surrounding region are also home to a rich history and culture, offering a unique glimpse into the past and present of Ecuador. From the ancient ruins and colonial architecture, to the vibrant local markets and delicious cuisine, there is always something new and exciting to experience on a day trip from Quito.

Don't miss out on the chance to explore the best things to do in Quito – watch our video to discover the top things to do and see around the city.

videos.transcript: here are the best things to do in Quito Ecuador be sure to stay until the end because we'll reveal the things many tourists Miss during their visit number 16. explore the UNESCO historic Old Town quito's historic old town was declared a UNESCO world heritage site in 1978 but that doesn't even begin to describe just how beautiful and significant this area is to the people of Ecuador located right in the heart of Quito this historic old town is full of landmarks such as the City Museum and La Merced Basilica along with centuries-old churches museums and restaurants it's truly a great place for soaking up the rich culture and history of Ecuador even if you are not into history it will not take you long to appreciate the beauty of colonial buildings if you stay here then rest assured you'll see something new every time you walk down the street it's easy to find your way around because everything is within walking distance when you're tired just sit at one of the many outdoor cafes with a coffee cup in your hand and watched the street musicians play number 15. have a Kane lasso at La Ronda you might not think of Quito as a place to enjoy an exotic drink but that's because you haven't tried the canalaso a popular drink throughout the country it's similar to a martini with gin substituted for our Dente a sugar cane alcohol but the fruit juice cinnamon and the common nadanhia flavor has its own distinct profile and deserves its own Spotlight if you're looking for the best canelaso in the city head to La Ronda and you'll find tables at second level balconies offering both beautiful views of the street and refreshing cocktails like this one make sure to visit at night if you're looking to enjoy your drink in a party atmosphere number 14. take a cable car up a volcano the teleferico is one of the top tourist activities in Quito with its cable cars whizzing people up a volcano for an amazing view of the city but the telefed Eco has more than just beautiful views to offer it's also an exciting ride on its own the 18-minute ride takes passengers up to an altitude of about 4 000 meters at the top of pinchinche volcano we recommend going on a sunny morning to get a nice and clear view and to skip the afternoon crowd number 13. spend a day bird watching in mindo mindo cloud forest is one of the most biodiverse places on Earth and it's not hard to see why when you go bird watching there there are over 500 species of birds and depending on when you visit you might see some of them tourists visit this place specifically to spot the and Dan of the rock bird also known as guyo De La Pena it's a beautiful red bird famous for its strange appearance and bouncing bobble-like head you can also join a birding tour with a local guide for a better experience don't forget to bring your binoculars to spot the parrots toucans quetzals and umbrella birds number 12 go to the equator in the middle of the world the middle of the world Monument near Quito should be on your bucket list as it's one of the most interesting attractions in the world the monument is located exactly on the world's equator and is marked by a yellow line it gives you the opportunity to stand on both hemispheres at the same time with one foot in the north and one foot in the South so don't forget to take a picture there the monument is located in a park with green spaces trees and flowers there are also restaurants nearby and shops that sell handicrafts and souvenirs for tourists at night it's lit up with different colors to represent the Earth's 24-hour cycle and there's an entrance fee to the park that would cost you five dollars number eleven visit the largest winged Virgin Mary statue the Statue of the Virgin Mary standing on top of a mountain is a powerful religious sculpture known as the verhen Del panaceo at 45 meters tall it is the largest winged Virgin Mary statue in the world it's found atop El panaceo the hill that overlooks all of central Quito when you visit be sure to bring your camera to capture the amazing views of the city and snap some selfies onto to the wings of this intriguing sculpture the best time to visit is in the early morning before it gets too hot and you'll enjoy a more Serene experience without crowds of tourists make sure to visit the Museum located within the structure where you can learn how the pieces were brought from France and how the statue was built number 10 take a selfie with the keto sign you've heard of the famous Hollywood sign but what about the keto sign located at the top of ichimbia hill this sign has become one of the most iconic landmarks in Quito it's a healthy climb up the many flights of stairs on the Eastern side of quito's Old Town to reach the area known as ichimbia Park the view from this spot is absolutely breathtaking you won't want to miss this fantastic photo opportunity number nine hike up kotoposi volcano and bike down kotoposi volcano sits in the heart of Ecuador's and Dan mountains and is the second highest peak in the country hiking this Trail offers breathtaking views at every turn the hike can be challenging and you need to have some acclimatization and glacier hiking experience however it takes just two days to reach the summit an easier option is to take a smaller hike to the snow line and have some hot cocoa at the Refugio biking downhill is a popular activity on the kotoposi volcano the ride down is an adrenaline pumping experience almost as if you're on a roller coaster feeling every bump in undulation of the road the ride offers mesmerizing and breathtaking views at every turn you might also spot some wild horses along the way the bike ride ends at the Laguna limpio Pungo which is a pretty Lake you should definitely check out number eight admire La floresta's street art La Floresta has an abundance of graffiti art murals and beautiful wall paintings all over the place this area is known by locals as an open air museum because of the dozens of artists that frequent it you won't want to miss this eclectic mix of art which will leave your mouth hanging open in shock and awe you can take a free walking tour of La Floresta led by a local street artist that'll show you all the notable places in the street or you can check out the several artist workshops and markets in the area in La Floresta street art is not just something to look at but an important way for residents to express themselves and share their culture with others number seven hang out at the Rooftop Bar at Secret Garden Quito you could say that the secret garden hostel in Quito has everything you'd ever want in a hostel including affordable rooms and amazing food but its standout feature is definitely the rooftop bar with Incredible views of the city it's the perfect place to meet with other Travelers and relax on your vacation the cool and calm Ambience of this bar is great for enjoying drinks with friends each evening everyone eats around the long dinner table which offers a great opportunity to meet new people to encourage actual conversation the Wi-Fi in this area of the hostel is turned off after 6 PM even if you do not stay in the hostel you're welcome to the Rooftop Bar number 6. Marvel at the golden Church the lock accompania Church houses one of the most impressive collections of baroque art in all of South America entering the church you would be greeted by a dazzling spectacle of gold carvings Gold Leaf display and golden columns lining the walls the altar also features a gold-leaved statue of Jesus Christ with painted scenes marveling at this golden church is like stepping back in time to the 16th century number five meet the president of Ecuador at the presidential Palace The Changing of the Guard ceremony in Quito takes place every Monday at 11 A.M it occurs outside the presidential Palace and here you'll get to see a small portion of Ecuador soldiers in action the event begins with vibrantly dressed Palace guards opening the doors of the palace to a marching band playing music accompanied by a whole Cavalry it's an interesting spectacle as the president himself joins the event and you'll get to see him waving to the crowd a free walking tour of the palace is also offered throughout the week except on Mondays the tour takes about 2 two hours and you'd be able to check out the different offices Courtyards balconies and rooms of the palace number four ride Achieva to celebrate fiestas dequito fiestas de Quito celebrates the foundations of the city of Quito and it takes place on the first week of December to celebrate this important event the people of Quito take to the streets in colorful costumes eating drinking and partying with their loved ones if you're visiting Quito during fiestas de Quito and you want to get fully involved in the festivities there's no better way to do it than by taking a ride on a traditional chiva bus the chiva is a party on Wheels and they are often decorated with bright vibrant colors and loud music that can be heard blocks away in the middle of the bus you would even find a pole to dance on passengers are encouraged to dance and drink on board the bus often stops along the route for more drinking dancing and socializing number three shop at South America's largest outdoor Market otavalo Market is one of the largest outdoor Market in South America every day thousands of people flock to otavalu to shop eat and enjoy Ecuador's vibrant culture whether you're looking to buy crafts or food products there's a little something for everyone at otovalo Market you should visit the market on Wednesday and Saturday as that's when the market spreads into the surrounding streams the market can be an overwhelming experience with all the colors smells and sounds it's a sensory overload if you're looking for souvenirs from your travels this is the place to go you'll find everything from traditional clothing handmade jewelry to food and ignatics while you're there remember to pop by the nearby otavalo animal Market there are hundreds of animals spread out over many acres including horses cows chicks and guinea pigs for cooking while we wouldn't recommend buying animals here it is an eye-opening experience number two take a dip in the hot springs of papayakta the taramas de papayakta are famous for their healing qualities and constant temperature throughout the year it's an ideal spot spent the previous day hiking or doing strenuous physical activity the pools here are less crowded than those in central Quito and many people find the area more relaxing than the Capital City's hectic pace that's why locals come here to relax and enjoy the soothing Waters number one have a beer in a church quito's Basilica Del voto Nacional is an impressive feat of architecture it's the largest neo-gothic church in all of the Americas one thing you might not expect is to drink a beer inside the church but that's exactly what makes it one of the most unique places to visit in Ecuador in the Basilica you can enjoy both the sacred and the secular it has an impressive Church interior and incredible view of Quito from the rooftop and a great little cafe hidden high up in the tower that serves a refreshing and cheap beer you'll be able to enjoy the experience of drinking a beer while taking in the awe-inspiring architecture of this historic building after reaching the top of the church it's a great way to unwind while taking in the sights of keto click here for more tips while traveling in Ecuador [Music]

videos.transcript: "Uh you ready for this." [MUSIC] Question is, are you ready for the crowds, the chaos, the cops, the clergy, um, the canines? We take a step back in time nearly 500 years to South America's oldest capital city while taking 847 breathless steps up a hillside through a neighborhood where kidnappings, muggings and robberies are common. "Maybe it is dangerous." All to take in the breathtaking views of a gorgeous metropolis nestled in the foothills of the Andes. Explore the statues, the streets, the steeples and the peoples in this city of two million people. It's a lot to juggle. But we've mapped out an unforgettable day for you if you ever decide to make this trip too. Because you're going to be amazed by what we discovered... "Right here in, uh, where are we? Quito, Ecuador." [MUSIC] From the minute we were greeted at the Quito airport till the time we left about 24 hours later, it's all a blur. Especially the long drive into town. The loooooong drive into town can be up to an hour, so if you need a nap, good time to take one. Unless you want to stay awake to take in the old architecture, the new graffiti or any of the other sights and sounds of the highest capital city in the world. [MUSIC] The city sits more than 9,000 feet above sea level, so maybe it's not just a lack of sleep making us a little dizzy. As our driver finally drops us off at this unmarked, nondescript wood door... where inside we are greeted with one of the most unique hotel experiences we've ever seen. [MUSIC] It's called Hotel La Basilica Quito, an old bed and breakfast built around an interior courtyard. With, it seems, a very unique history. "See this. Before being a garden it was storage for weapons. Every type of weapon was stored here, into the top part." We'll tell you more about this hotel in a future episode of Window Seat but suffice it to say, it is one of a kind. Oh and we do want to show you this one more thing: this spiral staircase to the rooftop, where you get a breathtaking view of the city. "This is the old city of Quito. This is the conquered city." [MUSIC] Now it's our turn to conquer this city. And the best way to do that is from the top. So that's what we decide to do, when off in the distance, we spot that silver behemoth glistening on a hilltop in the hot Quito sun. Taller than Christ the Redeemer in Rio and looming just as large. Just one thing separates us and this famous tourist attraction: about 800 steep dog-poop covered steps. [MUSIC] Uh, do we have to do this? "Alright, so we're about to begin our ascent. First steps right in front of us. Uh you ready for this? As ready as I'll ever be mate." Yes we do have to do this. These steps were laid out in front of us for a reason. And that reason? Well hell, I don't know! We're too out of breath to think about it. "The more you have me talk while we're walking up the stairs, the more tired I get." Alright, so here's what we didn't know going into this: these have been called the most dangerous stairs in Quito, where violent muggings, robberies and kidnappings are commonplace. But look at us! The uninformed American tourists, bounding up the stairs. "Right now we're at 370. Uh, feeling every bit of it. Uh but it's cool. It's worth it so far." Or struggling up the stairs with barely a care in the world. "Maybe it is dangerous." Honestly it didn't even occur to us that safety might be a problem until we realized we were literally the only ones on these stairs. And then we noticed the building started to get a little more covered in graffiti. And then we noticed all the wild dogs we had to step over. We just assumed, heck, these stairs are too hard for people so they just give up. "Yeah you can tell a lot of people start on the path but it looks like not as many people finished, because you get to the top. It's overgrown with weeds. There's a lot of dog poop everywhere. And overgrown with bushes here too. So my guess is people give up along the way but uh we're going for it." [MUSIC] And we do keep going for it. With each step, those stenciled numbers painted on the concrete go higher and higher until we reach 800 plus steps. That's when we finally run into three others locals who make us aware of the stupid mistake we just made. "Alright we may have figured out why it's not very busy at the top here. This group of guys just told us that it's really not very safe. As you get higher on the steps it gets more narrow, more places to get robbed or mugged so we're gonna stick with them for the rest of the route." [MUSIC] Maybe it was the Virgin Mary watching over us and protecting us, because we somehow made it safely to her feet... and to one of the most popular tourist spots in all of Quito. [MUSIC] The statue is called the Virgin of el Panecillo, which translates to "small bread," because of the shape of the hill it sits on. Built in 1976 by an artist commissioned by the Catholic church, the virgin is the biggest aluminum statue in the world. As we said, it stands taller than Christ the Redeemer in Rio de Janeiro, and it appears to be watching out over the city of Quito. "We're not done climbing quite yet. We still have a little ways to go." We decided to go all the way to the observation deck of the statue, 100 plus feet above the ground, where we get a truly astonishing view of this city and the historic old town off in the distance. I'll shut up and just let you look. [MUSIC] Back inside the statue, beautiful stained glass windows depicting characters and stories from the Bible and a museum of sorts, telling the history of the city and the statue. [MUSIC] On our way out of here we're faced with the dilemma: how do we safely get back to town? Do we dare take the steps again? Ah what the heck. We're idiots. And we spotted two other tourists on the path too. "What are we doing mate? Following them. Strength in numbers." [MUSIC] Now that our life is no longer on the line, it's time to get a real taste of what this city is like. The crowded streets, the fungussed feet? You can find a little of anything in this town, and a slew of people willing to sell it to you. It is easy to get overwhelmed by the crowds in this bustling city, and it's easy to worry yourself sick that you're going to be the victim of a crime. But the reality is, the police here are very proactive about warning you to keep your head on a swivel, and hide your expensive stuff. "They're very mindful of tourists. You have to appreciate it. If we're walking around with cameras here and we even had like the tourist police, they actually have tourist police, who come up to you and say be very careful holding your camera because someone might come snatch it. These are things we've kind of heard a lot of from different folks, but to actually have like a tourism police force actually come and tell you that, it's kind of interesting and it's very helpful in many ways." [MUSIC] And because of that diligence you can take a deep breath. Which ain't easy at this altitude. And enjoy everything this city has to offer... from the famed Independence Square in the heart of old Quito, home to the presidential palace, beautiful fountains and fired up preachers! As they yell fire and brimstone, you are surrounded in this city by all things biblical. "And you can definitely tell that religion plays a big role here in Quito. You have a lot of religious symbols everywhere you go." Seems every corner has a different cathedral on it. We walked into this one called The Church of the Tabernacle near Independence Square. Gorgeous, glowing and gilded... and a great place to stop for a moment of reflection. [MUSIC] Walk a few blocks and you'll find another cathedral and another town square and another reason to fall in love with the art and architecture of this amazing city. This one is called Plaza Santo Domingo. But the best church we found by far was the famous Basilica d.el Voto Nacional, where we met up with an old friend. "Hey look who's here!" Andy joined us after a work trip in Rio to take in the sights of Quito, and there's no better place to do that than from the top of this church. With its stunning 377 foot tall towers and a gorgeous observation deck where you can take in all the sights of this city." [MUSIC] Built on a hill in the northeastern part of old town. It's a gigantic gothic church that took 30 years to build starting in the 1890s. And the tour gives you a unique view of just how it was built. They recently added this really cool wooden plank walkway above the main cathedral sandwiched between the roof and the basilica ceiling where you can look down on those beautiful buttresses. And isn't that a fun thing to say... beautiful buttresses. [MUSIC] Alright by now, hours in the hot sun have passed. We're barely awake from our 4 a.m flight in this morning. So why not pound a few beers eh? Not like that would put us further to sleep or anything, right? But we did muster the strength to visit a cool place called Bandito Brewing, with a great selection of craft beers. And we capped off the day walking the streets some more, visiting a makeshift market where local souvenirs are sold. And then we decided more beers were in order. So we went back to Independence Square where we found a sidewalk cafe with cold beers and good local food. And we were treated to a cool performance by a bunch of clowns and jugglers and dancers and whatnot. [MUSIC] We had just 24 hours in Quito and I really wish we did have more. I hope you do have more if you come here. Yes there are dangers, and there's crime. And as we learned from taking those dangerous stairs, you've got to be careful not to put yourself in a bad situation. But this city is full of lively, happy, carefree people living it up in their gorgeous surroundings, proud of their hometown. And if you ask them about danger, they'd probably worry more about experiencing it if they visited America than they ever do right here. [MUSIC] Well that's it for this edition of Window Seat. Hope you liked what you saw. If you did, please hit the subscribe button. And don't forget to like and share too. We're back with another episode next week. In the meantime we hope you enjoy one of these other episodes from Window Seat as we attempt to travel to every country on the planet. See you next time! [MUSIC]

videos.transcript: here are the six best day trips from Quito Ecuador be sure to stay till the end because we'll reveal an underrated day trip that you'd hate to miss out on number one visit the kotoposi National Park kotoposi National Park offers some of the most amazing scenery in all of Ecuador including the snow-covered kotoposi volcano top and its red midsection it's famous for being the second highest mountain in Ecuador and is also known as the second largest active volcano in the world the park has it all from lavish Greenery to beautiful seasonal lakes and mesmerizing views of the Andes mountain range you can enjoy hours of hiking through diverse Landscapes spotting exotic animals in their natural habitats or simply relaxing next to the Lakes it also offers many opportunities for outdoor activities for example you can bike down the slopes of kotoposi volcano for a thrilling ride go for a short horseback riding experience in the Andes or just simply bird watching on a nearby Trail but by far the best way to explore the kotoposi National Park is by hiking there are a few trails that are accessible to you including a hike to the summit hiking up isn't an easy task that's why we recommend taking a local guide with you make sure you have all the equipment and necessary gear for hiking some sections of the trail can be quite Rocky so it's good to wear a nice pair of hiking boots take plenty of water with you and a first aid kit just in case number two visit the breathtaking kilo TOA Crater Lake kilotoa located in the Andes Mountains of central Ecuador is one of the most beautiful lakes you will ever see and it's hard to believe that it's completely natural its water comes from melting snow not rainfall which makes it even more surprising because only about six percent of kilotoa's surrounding mountains are made up of glaciers you can hike the caldetas trails to arrive at the turquoise Waters and Marvel at the hundreds of of nearby cacti in bloom the best time to visit kilatoa lake is between April and September when it's least likely to be foggy or rainy and also least likely to have tour groups from nearby cities there at the same time as you you can also experience this spot on foot as that way you'd be able to enjoy the sights and sounds around you as you explore the hike starts at the Viewpoint and goes down to the end of the surface Lake you need to be very careful as the Sandy soft Trail surface is very slippery and it's crowded with mules if you don't want to go through the tedious task of hiking and still want to enjoy the mesmerizing views of the water then make sure to check out the great Viewpoint located right near the parking area as it'll give you a breathtaking view of the full Caldera be sure to wear layers especially when you're hiking as it gets quite chilly even in the summer consider bringing lots of snacks water and sunscreen as the restaurants near the Viewpoint can be quite pricey by the way if you want to learn how to save money on airfares then click the Link in the description below this guide will teach you how to always get the cheapest flights number three visit ciudad mitar del mundo the middle of the world Monument near keto is a must-see site for you no matter if you're an avid traveler or not the middle of the world Monument near Quito should be on your bucket list as it's one of the most interesting attractions in the world the monument is located exactly on the world's equator and is marked by a yellow line it gives you the opportunity to stand on both hemispheres at the same time with one foot in the north and one foot in the South so don't forget to take a picture there it might be a bit hard for you to get a picture without someone being in the background and that's why we recommend visiting ciudad mitad del mundo in the morning as most of the tour groups and crowd comes after lunch the monument is located in a park with green spaces trees and flowers there are also restaurants nearby and shops that sell handicrafts and souvenirs for tourists at 9 night it's lit up with different colors to represent the Earth's 24-hour cycle and there's an entrance fee to the park that would cost you five dollars number four take a stroll through otavalo Market the otavalo market is one of the largest markets in South America this historic Market is where people from all over Ecuador as well as tourists from around the world come to shop at the thousands of clothing craft and food stands that line its streets and Alleyways the market and surrounding area offers you an opportunity to soak up the local culture and the hours long lines to enter the market can be intense even more so during Peak tourist season so don't be discouraged the inside will not disappoint you the majority of Artisans are indigenous people from the highlands of the Andes Mountains the otovalo market dates back to pre-hispanic times when communities of indigenous people from the Andes came to trade their Wares in this region of Northern Ecuador it's open every day but on when Wednesdays and Sundays you would find the Stalls spread around the town outside the main plaza you will see men here wearing hats and long braids while women will be wearing their traditional dress which is a white blouse embroidered with flowers handmade alpaca wool sweaters and hats are some of the common items you can buy they make for great souvenirs and they keep you warm the leather goods sold in this region are also very popular with both locals and tourists alike so you might try them as well the market is bustling with people and it's a joy to walk around and see what each vendor has to offer number five visiting the payuche waterfall looking to get away from the hustle and bustle of city life then the stunning payuche waterfall in otavalu is just what you need this magnificent waterfall creates its magic by tumbling down the Cliffs of the kotopaxi volcano bringing with it an amazing amount of peace and serenity when you take a swim in it on a hot day the waterfall is a great place to relax and admire the surrounding scenery but be sure to stay respectful as the waterfall is considered sacred among the indigenous people it's an all year round destination but the best time to visit this waterfall is either after a heavy rain spell or during the rainy season when the Falls are at their strongest unlike many tourist attractions there is no entrance fee here number six visit mindo if you're looking to be surrounded by some of the Region's most stunning natural beauty mindo could be your heaven on Earth also known as the bird watchers Paradise this isn't a place that should be passed through on your way somewhere else it should be the destination itself you can fully experience the beauty of the cloud forest by going on a guided tour and learning about the types of trees and their uses no matter how many times you've seen trees before there's something about the Lush foliage and towering trees that will take your breath away in addition to stunning views of greenery and trees you'd see plenty of birds flying by or resting on branches you might even get lucky and spot a colorful sloth hanging from a tree limb or two moreover if you're an adventure lover looking to experience something new while visiting mindo you'll find that there are plenty of ways to zip and glide through the Treetops of this gorgeous site it's an adventurous Sports Heaven that would give you a thrilling experience while the mesmerizing views across the mountains make your adventure even more worth it after you've got that Adrenaline Rush it's time to relax your body and mind by taking a butterfly tour here you'll see tons of different species of butterflies up close and learn about their life cycles and habitats meanwhile it's great news for chocolate lovers because at mindo you can take a tour through the chocolate factories where you can see the step-by-step process of how chocolate is made plus you get to sample some of these goodies too it will be a sweet treat that you won't soon forget click here for more tips while traveling in Ecuador
